title: Autonomous cross-scope secrets management
abstract: Various methods and systems are provided for autonomous orchestration of secrets renewal and distribution across scope boundaries. A cross-scope secrets management service ("SMS") can be utilized to store, renew and distribute secrets across boundaries in a distributed computing environment such as regional boundaries. In some embodiments, locally scoped secrets management services subscribe to receive updates from the cross- scope secrets management service. As secrets are renewed, they are automatically propagated to a subscribing local scope and distributed by the local secrets management service. In various embodiments, SMS can autonomously rollover storage account keys, track delivery of updated secrets to secrets recipients, deliver secrets using a secure blob, and/or facilitate autonomous rollover using secrets staging. In some embodiments, a service is pinned to the path where the service's secrets are stored. In this manner, secrets can be automatically renewed without any manual orchestration and/or the need to redeploy services.
claims:
Claims 1. A computer system comprising: one or more hardware processors and memory configured to provide computer program instructions to the one or more hardware processors; a cross-scope secrets management service (ГўВЂВњcross-scope SMSГўВЂВќ) configured to utilize the one or more hardware processors to: receive an input specifying secrets comprising a primary storage account key and a secondary storage account key for a deployed service and a rollover policy specifying interleaving expirations of the primary and secondary keys; install the secrets in a first secrets store of the cross-scope SMS; designate one of the storage account keys with a farther expiration as an active key; and automatically renew the secrets by automatically renewing a remaining one of the storage account keys in the first secret store pursuant to the specified rollover policy; and a local secrets management service (ГўВЂВњlocal SMSГўВЂВќ) configured to utilize the one or more hardware processors to: receive an input specifying a path of the secrets in the first secret store of the cross-scope SMS; fetch the secrets from the cross-scope SMS; distribute the secrets to facilitate an initial deployment of the service to a local scope of the local SMS in a distributed computing environment; fetch the renewed secrets by periodically polling the cross-scope SMS; and in response to receiving a periodic polling request from a container within the local scope associated with the service, release the renewed secrets to the container. 2. The computer system of claim 1, wherein the local SMS is further configured to track a local rollover of the renewed secrets in the local scope of the local SMS, wherein tracking comprises: setting a first local rollover state indicating the local rollover has begun; and setting a second local rollover state indicating the local rollover is 

 complete, in response to determining that a corresponding internal state for each of a plurality of agents associated with the service in the local scope indicates delivery of the renewed secrets has been confirmed. 3. A method for managing secrets across scope boundaries, the method comprising: receiving, at a cross-scope secrets management service (ГўВЂВњcross-scope SMSГўВЂВќ), a specified secret for a deployed service and a rollover policy for the secret; installing the secret in a first secrets store of the cross-scope SMS; in response to the cross-scope SMS receiving an initial polling request from a local secrets management service (ГўВЂВњlocal SMSГўВЂВќ), releasing the secret to the local SMS to facilitate an initial deployment of the service to a local scope of the local SMS in a distributed computing environment; automatically renewing the secret in the first secret store, by the cross-scope SMS, pursuant to the specified rollover policy; and in response to the cross-scope SMS receiving a periodic polling request from the local SMS, releasing the renewed secret to the local SMS to facilitate distribution of the renewed secret to the local scope by the local SMS. 4. The method of claim 3, the method further comprising, in response to the cross-scope SMS receiving an on-demand fetch request from the local SMS to obtain a most recent version of the secret, providing the most recent version by the cross-scope SMS to the local SMS. 5. The method of claim 3, wherein the service is pinned to a path of the secret in the first secrets store. 6. The method of claim 3, further comprising tracking, with the cross-scope SMS, a cross-scope rollover of the renewed secret, wherein tracking the cross-scope rollover comprises: setting a first rollover state indicating the cross-scope rollover has begun; and setting a second rollover state indicating the cross-scope rollover is complete, in response to determining that each of a plurality of local secrets management services associated with the service, including the local SMS, has completed a corresponding local rollover. 7. The method of claim 3, further comprising tracking, with the cross-scope SMS, a local rollover of the renewed secret associated with the local SMS, wherein tracking comprises: setting a first local rollover state indicating the local rollover has begun; and setting a second local rollover state indicating the local rollover is complete, in response to determining that a corresponding internal state for each of a plurality of agents associated with the service in the local scope indicates delivery of the renewed secret has been confirmed. 8. One or more computer storage media storing computer-useable instructions that, when used by one or more computing devices, cause the one or more computing devices to perform operations comprising: receiving, by a regional secrets management service (ГўВЂВњregional SMSГўВЂВќ), an input specifying a path of a secret in a first secret store of a cross-regional secrets management service (ГўВЂВњcross-regional SMSГўВЂВќ); receiving the secret, by the regional SMS, from the cross-regional SMS; distributing the secret, by the regional SMS, to facilitate an initial deployment of an associated service to a regional scope of the regional SMS in a distributed computing environment; periodically polling the cross-regional SMS, by the regional SMS, for an automatically renewed version of the secret stored in the first secret store; receiving the renewed secret, by the regional SMS, from the cross-regional SMS; and in response to the regional SMS receiving a periodic polling request from a container within the regional scope associated with the service, releasing the renewed secret to the container. 9. The media of claim 8, wherein the secret comprises a first key of a storage account, and wherein during a local rollover of the first key within the local scope, the local SMS falls back to an alternative key of the storage account. 10. The media of claim 8, wherein the secret comprises a storage account key, and wherein receiving the renewed secret comprises receiving a secrets package comprising regenerated shared access keys.
